  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Severe water hammer (hydraulic shock) occurs immediately after the fixture valve closes.",
    "Lack of adequate pipe securing, strapping, or water hammer arrestors."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install a water hammer arrestor close to the fast-closing valve.",
    "Secure loose PEX and copper piping with proper hangers and straps to prevent movement."
  ],
  "rationale": "The audio clearly demonstrates a loud, repetitive banging sound (water hammer) immediately following the rapid closure of a fixture valve. This hydraulic shock can damage joints, fittings, and appliances over time, and violates standard plumbing codes regarding piping support and pressure surge control.",
  "references": [
    "IPC Section 604.9 (Water hammer)",
    "IPC Section 308 (Piping support)"
  ]
}
